meta.description: Reorganizes a paper into a storyline suitable for scientific posters. Plans section structure, title hierarchy, figure selection, and live-explanation flow for academic conference posters.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated results or conclusions. Hard rules explicitly prohibit recommending figures not mentioned in the user's study and fabricating results not in the source material.

practice_boundaries.detail: No clinical advice or prescriptive recommendations. Scope limited to poster layout planning.

methodological_ground.detail: No methodological claims. Poster layout advice is evidence-based communication design rather than scientific methodology.

code_usability.detail: No executable code generated; Mode A direct-execution skill.

functional_suitability.note: 5-step workflow covers story extraction, figure selection, text compression, layout plan, and design tips. Space allocation table and per-section word count targets are specific and actionable. Minor deduction: no mention of conference-specific poster format variations (portrait/landscape, A0/A1, digital vs physical) that meaningfully affect layout decisions.

reliability.note: Input validation correctly triggers follow-up questions when source material is too sparse. Hard rules prevent figure fabrication. Minor deductions: no explicit flagging when primary result is unquantified (affects figure selection quality), and font size recommendations are not verified against specific conference templates.

performance_context.note: Full marks. SKILL.md is compact (~133 lines). Reference file is a stub (3 lines). Step 1 story extraction runs before layout plan generation, which is efficient.

agent_usability.note: Trigger phrases are specific and cover all major entry points. Poster section structure ASCII diagram is highly readable. Minor deductions: no confirmation step between story extraction and full layout plan delivery (user cannot correct Step 1 interpretation before Step 4 output), and no explicit guidance for when user provides too many figures to prioritize.

human_usability.note: Strong 'When to Use' section with 5 specific scenarios. Out-of-scope note about design tools prevents scope confusion. Minor deduction: no clear path described when user has only a partial manuscript (e.g., abstract only, no methods or figure list).

security.note: Full marks. Hard rules explicitly prohibit fabricating results and recommending unmentioned figures. No credential or injection risks.

maintainability.note: references/guidelines.md is a stub (3 lines, no substantive content). All functional content is inline in SKILL.md without modular separation. Low modularity — adding conference-specific guidelines would require editing SKILL.md directly. Significant maintainability deduction.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision is excellent. Progressive disclosure via 5 steps prevents output overload. Idempotent: same abstract → same layout plan. Minor deductions: no composability with graphical-abstract-generator or slide-deck-for-lab-meeting despite natural workflow adjacency; and escape hatch for unknown conference format is not explicitly described.

inputs.label: Phase 2 RCT poster for ASH: BTKi vs control in relapsed/refractory CLL (ORR 78% vs 45%, p<0.001, n=120, 36×48 inch portrait). Figures: KM curves, waterfall plot, AE table.
inputs.note: Step 1 correctly extracts all 5 story elements. Figure plan: KM curves (must-include primary result), waterfall plot (should-include), AE table (convert to visual bar chart or summarize in text). Layout plan with 4-column structure, word count targets per section, take-home sentence, and design tips produced.

assertions.text: Step 1 correctly identifies all 5 story elements (problem, objective, approach, answer, implication)
assertions.note: CLL unmet need, BTKi ORR objective, phase 2 RCT design, 78% vs 45% finding, and treatment implication all correctly extracted.

assertions.text: Figure plan recommends 2-4 figures maximum with clear must-include/should-include/cut guidance
assertions.note: Step 2 figure selection applied: KM curves (must-include), waterfall plot (should-include), AE table (recommended to convert or summarize).

assertions.text: Section plan includes word count targets per the text compression table
assertions.note: Step 3 word count targets correctly applied to all sections.

assertions.text: Take-home sentence is quantitative and specific (includes primary endpoint numbers)
assertions.note: Take-home includes ORR numbers rather than generic 'drug X improves outcomes.'

assertions.text: No results or conclusions fabricated beyond what was provided in the input
assertions.note: Hard rule enforced; layout plan based entirely on user-provided data.

inputs.label: Basic science poster for AACR: CRISPR screen identifying PARP1 as synthetic lethal partner with BRCA2 in TNBC (10-fold olaparib sensitization). Figures: volcano plot, cell viability curves, western blot.
inputs.note: Layout plan adapted for basic science: Methods column given more space than clinical RCT (novel CRISPR methodology). Volcano plot as primary results/methods figure, viability curves as main result, western blot as supporting. Minor: layout does not explicitly note that AACR-specific size requirements should be verified.

assertions.text: Story extracted correctly for basic science format (biological problem, mechanistic objective, experimental approach, key finding, translational implication)
assertions.note: Step 1 adapted correctly for basic science vs clinical study framing.

assertions.text: Methods section allocated more poster space than standard clinical RCT layout due to novel CRISPR methodology
assertions.note: Space allocation correctly adapted for methods-heavy basic science poster.

assertions.text: Figure plan prioritizes cell viability curves as primary result figure with volcano plot as context
assertions.note: Must-include correctly identified as the figure showing the quantitative sensitization result.

assertions.text: Layout plan notes that AACR conference-specific poster format should be verified
assertions.note: No conference-specific verification note included. General A0 format used without flagging potential AACR format requirements.

assertions.text: No figures recommended beyond the three user-specified figures
assertions.note: Hard rule against recommending unmentioned figures correctly enforced.

inputs.label: Sparse input: 'Help me make a poster about my research on immune cells and cancer'
inputs.note: Hard rule triggered: source material too sparse to create a layout. Focused questions asked covering primary result with numbers, available figures, conference/size, and primary audience. No layout plan produced from vague 'immune cells and cancer' topic.

assertions.text: Skill correctly withholds layout plan from topic-only sparse input
assertions.note: Hard rule ('if source material is too sparse, ask for key finding and methods before proceeding') correctly applied.

assertions.text: Focused questions cover primary result (with numbers), available figures, conference format, and audience type
assertions.note: Step 2 figure selection and Step 1 story extraction prerequisites correctly identified.

assertions.text: No fabricated immune cell or cancer results generated from the vague input
assertions.note: Hard rule against fabricating results enforced.

assertions.text: Response explains what information is needed and why each piece matters for layout planning
assertions.note: Users understand why specificity is required for a useful layout plan.

assertions.text: Provisional section structure offered as a starting template for common oncology posters
assertions.note: Generic template offered as a provisional starting point while awaiting specific content.

key_strengths: Space allocation guidelines (Results 35-45%, Introduction 10-15%) are practically calibrated and rare in academic communication tools
Figure selection strategy (Must-include/Should-include/Cut-for-poster) converts vague figure priority decisions into actionable guidance
Text compression table with per-section word count targets gives users measurable goals rather than generic advice
Design accessibility tip (colorblind-safe color scheme, avoid red/green) addresses a commonly overlooked poster quality issue

recommendations.title: references/guidelines.md is a stub with no substantive content — contributes nothing to skill quality
recommendations.problem: The only reference file contains 3 lines ('Academic Poster Guidelines', 'Visual Design Principles') with no actual content. This creates false confidence about the skill's reference coverage and contributes nothing to output quality.
recommendations.root_cause: Reference file appears to be a placeholder that was never populated.
recommendations.fix: Either delete references/guidelines.md or replace it with substantive content: conference-specific poster format table (ASH, ASCO, AACR, ISMB common sizes and format requirements), colorblind-accessible color palette guidance, and font size requirements for print poster readability.

recommendations.title: No composability with graphical-abstract-generator or slide-deck-for-lab-meeting despite natural workflow adjacency
recommendations.problem: Poster planning is a natural precursor to graphical abstracts (same figure curation logic) and shares content with slide decks. Users who need both a poster and a talk frequently run these tasks sequentially, but no workflow guidance is provided.
recommendations.root_cause: Skill designed as a standalone tool without pipeline positioning.
recommendations.fix: Add a 'Position in Research Communication Pipeline' note: 'This skill creates the poster content plan. For graphical abstracts, use graphical-abstract-generator (shares the figure selection rationale). For conference talk slides, use slide-deck-for-lab-meeting (builds on the same storyline structure).'

recommendations.title: Conference-specific format variations not addressed — font and space recommendations may not match conference requirements
recommendations.problem: Layout plan uses A0/36×48 inch as default without flagging when conference-specific dimensions (e.g., ASCO 48×48 inch, ASH 44×44 inch, or digital e-poster formats) differ. Font size recommendations (72pt title, 36pt headers) are best practice but not conference-validated.
recommendations.root_cause: Design guidance is general rather than conference-specific.
recommendations.fix: Add a conference verification note at the start of Step 4: 'Font sizes and section proportions are calibrated for standard A0 or 36×48 inch posters. Always verify poster dimensions, font minimums, and mandatory sections against the specific conference poster guidelines before printing.'
